config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, mudah sekali disalahgunakan untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan melindungi hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat banyak kasus di Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” sering dipakai untuk menutup ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that says the common good automatically outranks personal rights, because that language has often been used here to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require limits. The real test is whether those limits are narrow, accountable, and genuinely necessary, not whether they simply sound beneficial to the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis lebih tinggi daripada hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, menggusur tanpa adil, atau membungkam kritik, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas yang terlalu luas seperti itu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru paling sehat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, dengan pembatasan hak individu hanya dalam keadaan yang benar-benar perlu, jelas dasar hukumnya, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau tidak ada batas yang tegas, “masyarakat” mudah dijadikan alasan untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that kind of broad claim is exactly what has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, forced conformity, and abuse of power under the banner of the common good. Public health, safety, and order can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, transparent, and subject to real oversight. If "society" becomes a blank check, then the rights of ordinary people are no longer rights at all, just privileges the majority can revoke.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup ketidakadilan, dari penggusuran sampai pembungkaman kritik, jadi saya tidak percaya pada konsep prioritas yang terlalu luas seperti itu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, dengan pembatasan yang benar-benar perlu, jelas aturannya, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan yang memberi cek kosong kepada penguasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. In the United States, we have seen too many cases where appeals to the "common good" were used to excuse censorship, discrimination, mass surveillance, and other abuses of power, so I am very suspicious of that framing. That said, I do not treat individual rights as absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require real limits. Those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable, not a vague excuse to let the majority overrule people whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
